Tests the behavior of Math.ceil with negative infinity in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test case that verifies the behavior of the `Math.ceil` function when applied to negative infinity. It asserts that the result of `Math.ceil(-Infinity)` is equal to `-Infinity`. This test ensures that the `Math.ceil` function correctly handles edge cases involving infinite values, which is important for maintaining mathematical consistency in applications that use this function. The code is narrow in scope, focusing specifically on this single mathematical operation.
